columns.name: CURRENT_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY
columns.description: Current energy rating (0–100, higher = more efficient)

columns.name: TOTAL_FLOOR_AREA
columns.description: Total floor area in square metres

columns.name: PROPERTY_TYPE
columns.description: Type of property (e.g., House, Flat, Bungalow)

columns.name: lsoa21cd
columns.description: Code for the small neighbourhood area where the property is (roughly village or suburb level). Carries the strongest signal for owner profile — income, occupation, age — because it is the most local.

columns.name: msoa21cd
columns.description: Code for a mid-sized area that covers several neighbourhoods (roughly small town or district level). Slightly less specific than neighbourhood for owner profile but still reflects local income and employment patterns.

columns.name: ladcd
columns.description: Code for the local council area that runs local services (e.g. Manchester, Bristol). The broadest level; reflects regional differences in owner income and demographics.

columns.name: label
columns.description: Whether the property sold (1) or not (0) — the real conversion outcome used for reference.

areaProfileExplanation: These area identifiers also capture information about typical owners and buyers — their income, occupation, age, and other demographics. Official statistics on deprivation, employment, and education are published at these area levels, and neighbourhoods tend to cluster by such factors. In practice, the smallest area (Neighbourhood) gives the sharpest picture of who lives there; the mid-sized area (District) is a bit less specific; the council area is the broadest. Together they act as proxies when we do not have direct household data.

metrics.name: ROC-AUC
metrics.description: Ability to rank high-potential leads above low-potential ones (0.5 = random, 1 = perfect)

metrics.name: PR-AUC
metrics.description: Quality of predictions when positives are rare (good for imbalanced data)

metrics.name: Brier
metrics.description: Average squared error of probabilities (lower = better calibrated)

metrics.name: Log loss
metrics.description: Penalty for confident wrong predictions (lower = better)

focusExplanation: Both models predict the likelihood that a property lead will convert (sell within a defined period). They use the same inputs (property features, efficiency, geography, etc.) but were tuned slightly differently: one emphasizes overall ranking (ROC-AUC), the other precision when positives are rare (PR-AUC). For demos, either score is suitable; the difference is small.
